skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error
stem: Two random samples of voters were drawn from the same population to estimate support for a ballot measure. Both samples used the same method for computing margin of error. Sample X had a larger margin of error than sample Y. Which of the following is the most appropriate reason for this?

answerOptions.content: Sample X had a smaller sample size.
Sample X had a larger sample size.
Sample X had a higher percent of favorable responses.
Sample X had a smaller number of invalid responses.

rationale: Holding all else equal, the margin of error is inversely proportional to the square root of the sample size, so a smaller sample produces a larger margin of error. Choice B is the opposite. Choices C and D have weaker, not primary, effects on margin of error relative to sample size.
